mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy in the face of inflationary pressures, which can boost equity valuations as real rates are suppressed, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the specified conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth during periods of low inflation suggests efficient supply-side expansion without overheating, leading to sustainable corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month average growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 2%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a certain level indicates a weakening housing market and broader economic demand, which typically precedes a slowdown in corporate activity and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 (thousands of units) for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1000 for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer confidence and spending, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly negative (more than 60% of observations) over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above a certain level signals aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs, slows economic activity, and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong GDP growth combined with robust retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, which provides a strong tailwind for corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 2% and RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3%
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong growth as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of such periods within an economic expansion regime.
